authoring_disclosure.fork: F-KN-A option (ii): LLM-drafted, then linter-gated, then human blind style spot-check at maintainer sign-off
authoring_disclosure.drafting_model_family: Claude family (Fable) - a different vendor and model family from the SmolLM2 host models, so host-model self-idiom leakage (design doc section 5 attack 9) is not same-family; the residual style-familiarity axis is measured by the truthstyle-2x2 sibling line
authoring_disclosure.register: plain dictionary-definition English (ordinary defining vocabulary, Latinate where natural); zero NSM-legal syntax, enforced by linter checks R-1..R-3 in poc/knull/lint_plain_store.py
authoring_disclosure.constraints_inherited_from_build: own-label LC1 (full label AND headword), +/-25 percent word band vs the NSM gloss, >=2 admissible claim segments (>=15 chars, no double quotes), pairwise uniqueness, ASCII only
authoring_disclosure.spot_check_file: poc/knull/inputs/plain-spotcheck.json (10 deterministically sampled definitions for the blind maintainer style check)

definitions.afraid: experiencing fear; a state of alarm in which a person expects harm or danger and wishes to avoid it; often accompanied by an urge to escape or hide
definitions.alive: in a living state rather than dead; possessing the biological processes that sustain an organism
definitions.angry: feeling or showing strong annoyance or hostility; a reaction to a perceived offense or injustice done by another person; often paired with an impulse to retaliate or protest
definitions.archived (bookmark boolean property): a status flag applied to a saved link record; when the flag is set, the record is moved out of the active reading list and stored away from current view; the item remains retrievable on demand, and the user can restore or consult it later whenever the need arises
definitions.begin (happening X begins at time T): to start occurring; the point at which a process or activity commences; prior to that point the activity was absent
definitions.believe (X believes Y): to accept a statement as accurate without possessing proof; holding a conviction that may or may not be verified
definitions.birth (the event): the occasion on which a new individual emerges from its mother and enters the world; the starting point of a creature's independent life
definitions.bookmark: a saved pointer that records the location of a document or page; created by a user so that the material can be reopened quickly; it carries a short descriptive title and spares the reader from searching again
definitions.break (X breaks Y): to damage an object with sudden force so that it separates into fragments; the object ceases to function or hold together; the damage is generally difficult to reverse and renders the item useless
definitions.broken: damaged so as to no longer work or hold together; in a state of disrepair following an earlier fracture or failure
definitions.cause (happening X causes happening Y): to bring about an effect or result; the earlier occurrence produces the later one, which would not otherwise arise
definitions.celebration: a festive gathering held to mark a happy occasion or achievement; participants share food, music, and enjoyment together; such gatherings often follow weddings, victories, holidays, or other welcome milestones
definitions.change (the event): the process by which a person or object becomes different; a shift from an earlier condition to a new condition; the prior state no longer fully applies afterward
definitions.condolence (the words): a message of sympathy offered to a person who is mourning a loss; expressed to ease sorrow and show that the speaker cares; commonly delivered after a bereavement in speech or writing
definitions.conversation: an informal spoken exchange between two or more people; each participant talks in turn and listens to the others; topics can range widely as remarks pass back and forth
definitions.dead: no longer living; having permanently ceased all vital functions; incapable of any further action or awareness
definitions.death (the event): the event that ends a creature's life; the moment at which vital functions permanently stop; afterwards the individual exists only as remains and cannot revive
definitions.end (happening X ends at time T): to stop occurring; the point at which a process or activity concludes; beyond that point the activity is finished and belongs to the past
definitions.event: an occurrence that takes place at a particular time; once finished it becomes part of the past and may be recalled
definitions.find (X finds Y): to discover the location of a person or object after searching; the searcher, previously unaware of where the item was, now perceives it and knows its position
definitions.forget (X forgets Y): to lose stored knowledge or information from the mind; material once familiar can no longer be recalled when needed
definitions.friend (X is a friend of Y): a person with whom one shares mutual affection and trust; the two enjoy spending leisure together and support each other; each hopes for the other's welfare and would regret seeing the other suffer
definitions.gift: an item presented voluntarily to another person without expectation of payment; ownership transfers to the recipient; the giver intends the present to bring pleasure or express goodwill toward the recipient
definitions.give (X gives Y to someone): to hand over possession of an item to another person; the item formerly belonged to the donor; after the transfer it belongs to the recipient instead, without payment required
definitions.grieving: mourning a recent bereavement; experiencing deep sorrow after the loss of a loved one; the sadness persists and occupies the mind for a period
definitions.happy: feeling pleasure and contentment; a cheerful state that arises when circumstances have gone well and nothing further is missed; frequently shown through smiles and lightness of mood
definitions.has part (X has part Y): to contain a component; the whole includes the named piece within its structure
definitions.help (X helps Y): to assist another person; the assistance enables that person to accomplish an intended task
definitions.helpful (of a someone): habitually providing assistance to others; a considerate disposition aimed at making tasks easier for other people and improving their wellbeing
definitions.inside (X is inside Y): located within the interior of a container; contents travel along whenever the container moves
definitions.kind (gufo:Kind, sortal type): a category that classifies an entity by its fundamental nature; membership is permanent and cannot be shed; the category supplies the criteria for counting an entity as a single individual and for recognizing it as the same one encountered previously
definitions.learn (X learns Y): to acquire knowledge or a skill through study and effort; the material, unknown beforehand, becomes known through deliberate practice
definitions.liar: a person who habitually tells falsehoods; repeated dishonesty leads others to distrust the speaker and to judge the speaker's character poorly
definitions.lie (the words): a statement the speaker knows to be false; uttered deliberately to mislead the listener into accepting it as fact; deception of this sort betrays the listener's confidence
definitions.lose (X loses Y): to misplace an object and become unable to say where it is; the owner previously knew its location and now wishes to recover it
definitions.lost: misplaced and currently untraceable; the owner cannot presently locate the missing item, though its whereabouts were known earlier
definitions.make (X makes Y): to create or produce an object; through deliberate work something is brought into existence that was absent before
definitions.maker of (X is the maker of Y): the agent who produced a particular object; through that agent's earlier work the object came into existence and now exists
definitions.memory (a stored something): a trace of past experience retained within a person's mind; the stored impression preserves information about an earlier object or occurrence; it allows the person to recall that material later and supports acts of remembering
definitions.near (X is near Y): positioned a short distance away; close enough that an observer standing at the reference point could readily see the neighboring object
definitions.part of (X is part of Y): a component belonging to a larger whole; removing the component would leave the whole incomplete and altered
definitions.promise (the words): a spoken or written commitment to carry out a stated action; the pledge is addressed to a person who desires that action; by giving it, the speaker invites the hearer to count on its fulfillment
definitions.remember (X remembers Y): to retain past knowledge and bring it back to mind; information gained earlier stays available for present recall
definitions.reminder (the made something): an object or note prepared so that a matter will not slip from attention; a person sets it up in advance of a future task; encountering it later prompts recollection at the proper moment; with such an aid in place, the intended matter is unlikely to be overlooked
definitions.repair (X repairs Y): to restore a damaged object to working order; the item, previously unusable, is mended through deliberate effort; once the work is complete the object functions again as intended
definitions.right (of a doable something): morally proper to perform; an action of this sort earns general approval, and people regard doing it as commendable conduct
definitions.sad: feeling sorrow or unhappiness; a low emotional state that follows an unwelcome development the person was powerless to prevent; often visible in a downcast expression and subdued manner
definitions.take (X takes Y): to seize possession of an item; the object belonged to someone else or to no one beforehand; after the act the new holder treats the item as belonging to them
definitions.teacher: a person whose occupation is instructing students; the instructor explains subjects so that learners gain knowledge and skills; much of the work consists of presenting lessons and guiding practice
definitions.thief: a person who repeatedly steals the property of others; such takings are unwelcome to the victims; the offender works furtively, hoping to escape notice and avoid being observed
definitions.trustworthy: reliably honest and dependable; a person of this character keeps commitments once made and avoids telling falsehoods; others accordingly form a favorable judgment and place their confidence in such a person
definitions.useful (of a something): serving a practical purpose; an item of this quality lets its owner accomplish desired tasks more easily and brings clear benefit whenever it is applied
definitions.visible: able to be seen; open to view, so that anyone who looks in the right direction perceives it
definitions.wrong (of a doable something): morally improper to perform; an action of this sort causes harm or offense, and people condemn those who choose to do it
definitions.animal: a living creature capable of movement and sensation; unlike a human being it cannot speak or reason; the class spans countless species, from household companions to wild creatures dwelling in remote regions; members perceive their surroundings through senses such as sight and hearing
definitions.ball: a manufactured plaything with a uniformly round shape; struck or thrown by hand, it rolls or bounces across the ground for a while; children spend hours playing games with one because the activity is enjoyable; its evenness distinguishes it from most other objects
definitions.bear: a large wild mammal with thick fur and great strength; it inhabits forests and remote wilderness rather than settled areas; a close encounter with one can prove gravely dangerous to a person
definitions.bird: a feathered animal, typically small, that flies through the air on wings; species are found nearly everywhere; the melodious calls of many varieties are pleasant for people to listen to
definitions.blue: the hue of a clear daytime heaven and of deep expanses of ocean; wavelengths of this shade sit between green and violet on the spectrum; commonly regarded as a cool and calming tone
definitions.book: a manufactured volume of printed pages bound together; it holds an extended text for readers to study or enjoy; by reading one, a person absorbs stories or information assembled by its author; libraries and shops supply them in great numbers for this reason
definitions.box: a manufactured container with flat rectangular sides and a lid; goods are placed within it for storage or transport; it can be opened and shut; while shut, the contents stay hidden from view
definitions.car: a manufactured road vehicle powered by an engine; it seats a driver and passengers within an enclosed cabin; rolling on four wheels, it carries its occupants along streets and highways, covering great distances in little time compared with travel on foot
definitions.cat: a small domesticated feline commonly kept as a household companion; it moves with silent steps and hunts by stealth; its sharp senses let it prowl effectively in darkness where human eyes fail
definitions.child: a young person in the early years of life; someone recently born, with a small frame and limited abilities; dependence on adult care is typical at this stage; with the passage of years the youngster matures into a capable grown person
definitions.close: to shut an opening or container; the reverse of opening it; passage in or out becomes blocked once the action is done; items previously reachable through the opening are sealed away until it is undone
definitions.clothes: garments manufactured to be worn on the human body; people dress in them for modesty, warmth, and style; fabric coverings shield the wearer from chill and conceal the figure from public gaze; wardrobes hold many types for different occasions
definitions.cold: a low temperature felt through the skin on contact or exposure; water and winter air often have this property; the opposite of the warmth given off by a flame; prolonged chilling of the whole body is harmful and can become dangerous without shelter
definitions.color: a visual property of surfaces perceived by the eye; hues such as red, green, and yellow are examples; two objects may match in this respect; a person without sight cannot judge the property; the range of distinguishable shades is vast
definitions.cry: to shed tears in response to strong distress; drops of moisture run from the eyes while the voice may sob audibly; onlookers who notice the weeping can tell that the person is suffering emotionally; the reaction is hard to suppress
definitions.day: the portion of each cycle during which the sun lights the land; people rise, work, and go about their activities in this bright interval; it alternates with the dark hours on a regular schedule, returning once every twenty-four-hour period throughout the year
definitions.dog: a domesticated canine widely kept by households; it barks loudly when anything stirs nearby, alerting owners at a distance; renowned for loyalty, it forms warm bonds with its keepers, and most owners return the affection; breeds vary greatly in size
definitions.eat: to take food into the mouth, chew it, and swallow it down into the stomach; the routine is repeated at meals throughout every day; nourishment absorbed this way keeps the body functioning; an individual deprived of nourishment for an extended stretch will starve and eventually perish
definitions.eye: one of the paired organs of sight set in the face; vision depends on them; serious injury to these organs leaves a person blind and unable to perceive light
definitions.father: a male parent; the man whose union with the mother led to a child's conception and arrival; such a parent commonly shares the home with his offspring over many years, providing care, support, and guidance while the youngsters grow
definitions.fire: the hot glowing process of combustion; flames flicker and rise as material burns; standing close, one feels the radiated heat; direct contact scorches flesh severely, and property caught in the blaze may be destroyed; it also provides warmth and light for people
definitions.fish: an aquatic animal that dwells and swims in water; gills let it breathe there; removed from its element for long, the creature quickly perishes
definitions.flower: the blossoming part of certain small rooted plants; each bloom lasts only a brief season before wilting; the shapes and colors delight those who look at them, so people cultivate gardens and arrange cut stems indoors to keep such beauty close at hand
definitions.food: edible material consumed to sustain life; people prepare and share meals of it daily; nourishment from what is eaten keeps the body alive and active; hunger drives much human effort, since starvation follows when supplies run out for long
definitions.grass: low green vegetation formed of countless slender blades; it carpets fields and lawns in dense profusion; walking barefoot across the soft turf is a pleasant sensation
definitions.ground: the solid surface underfoot upon which everything rests; feet press against it with every step; objects in a landscape stand upon or above it, while buried items lie concealed beneath the soil
definitions.hand: the grasping organ at the end of each arm; a person has a pair, one on the left and one on the right; fingers and a thumb allow it to seize, carry, and manipulate objects with precision; nearly every manual task, from writing to lifting, relies on these versatile organs
definitions.head: the uppermost part of the body, set atop the neck; it houses the brain, the organ of thought; the face with its eyes and mouth forms its front; hair typically covers its crown
definitions.house: a building constructed for people to dwell in; rooms within it shelter the residents and their belongings; walls and a roof keep out rain and weather, so the family inside stays dry when storms pass overhead
definitions.hug: an embrace in which one person wraps both arms around another and draws them together briefly; given to express warm affection toward the recipient; the gesture comforts and pleases the person receiving it, strengthening the bond between the two
definitions.jump: to spring off the ground by a quick push of the legs; for an instant the whole body hangs in the air with no support; gravity then returns the feet to the surface; athletes practice the movement for sport
definitions.laugh: an involuntary burst of merry sound from the mouth and chest; amusement or delight triggers it rather than deliberate choice; the cheerful noise carries to everyone nearby, and hearing it tends to lift the listeners' spirits as well; comedy aims to provoke it
definitions.light: the radiant energy that makes surroundings visible; illumination floods a space from the sun, from flames, or from manufactured lamps; wherever it reaches, objects can be seen; in its absence darkness hides everything from view; it travels outward from its source and reflects off surfaces into the observer's eyes
definitions.man: an adult male human being; a grown person who has lived through childhood and adolescence; males of the species do not bear offspring; men and women together make up the adult population
definitions.money: the tokens of currency used in trade; coins and banknotes pass from buyer to seller in exchange for goods and services; because purchases require them, people work hard to earn and save such tokens; wealth is measured by how much one holds
definitions.mother: a female parent; the woman from whose body a child was born; she typically raises the youngster in her home over many years, tending to its needs with deep affection and doing countless good turns on its behalf
definitions.mouth: the opening in the lower face used for speech and eating; lips frame it and it moves as words are spoken; food rests briefly within it, where the tongue tastes whatever enters
definitions.music: organized sound arranged in melody and rhythm for listeners' enjoyment; performers create it so that audiences may hear it; a stirring piece can move hearers deeply, prompting them to dance, hum, or sing along; concerts, recordings, and radio spread performances to audiences everywhere
definitions.night: the dark portion of each daily cycle, when the sun is absent from view; little can be seen outdoors without lamps; most people spend these hours asleep in their beds; dawn brings the bright period around again
definitions.open: to unfasten a container or barrier, often with the hand, so that access becomes possible; before the action, contents are sealed away and hidden; afterward, items can be put in or taken out freely, and anyone may look at what lies within; doors, lids, and jars all admit this treatment
definitions.picture: a crafted image depicting a scene, person, or object; produced by drawing, painting, or photography; viewers recognize in it the subject it portrays, and looking at one calls that absent subject to mind; framed examples decorate walls so distant sights stay present
definitions.rabbit: a small burrowing mammal with long upright ears; it travels in quick bounding hops; its gentle appearance charms most people who watch one
definitions.red: the vivid hue of blood and of glowing embers; flames often show it, and a bleeding wound displays it plainly; on signals and warning signs it marks danger or commands a stop; ripe tomatoes and berries wear the shade too
definitions.rock: a hard mineral mass found on and beneath the earth's surface; stones of the sort are inanimate and extremely durable; striking or squeezing one produces no change in it; specimens range from pebbles small enough to hold up to enormous boulders and cliffs
definitions.run: to move on foot at a rapid pace, faster than walking; the whole body works as both feet leave the surface in turn; a sprinting person covers considerable distance in moments
definitions.sit: to rest the body on a seat with the weight settled on the lower trunk; the posture is stationary, with the frame supported just above whatever bears it; people adopt the position when tired of standing, and remain settled that way through meals, work, and rest
definitions.sky: the vast expanse arching over the landscape; a person standing outdoors sees it stretching overhead; the sun crosses it by day, while stars and the moon appear in it after dusk; clouds drift through the expanse, and birds fly across it
definitions.sleep: the nightly state of rest in which the body lies still and awareness fades; a slumbering person does not see, barely hears, and knows nothing of events nearby; everyone requires the restorative pause, and drowsiness demands it after long exertion; dreams may visit during the deepest stretches, vanishing by morning
definitions.smile: a facial expression in which the corners of the lips curve upward; pleasure or friendliness usually prompts it; observers read the look as a sign that the person is pleased
definitions.sun: the brilliant celestial body that dominates the daytime heavens; it hangs immensely far above every land; its rays supply the daylight by which people see, and its warmth heats the earth; when it sets, darkness settles until it climbs into view again
definitions.tree: a tall long-lived plant with a woody trunk; roots anchor it in the soil while branches spread high overhead, visible from afar; it stands rooted in a single spot for its whole life; forests consist of great numbers of them growing together across the land
definitions.walk: to travel on foot at an unhurried pace; one foot stays planted while the other swings forward, so contact with the surface is never lost; step by step the traveler advances and in time reaches another place; it is the commonest way people get about
definitions.water: the clear liquid that fills rivers, lakes, and seas; it flows readily and splashes audibly when it moves; skin registers its wetness at a touch; every living body depends on regular drinks of the fluid, and going without it for days is fatal
definitions.woman: an adult female human being; a grown person, no longer a girl, who has reached maturity; individuals of this sex can bear children from their own bodies
